figures.caption: Figure 1: Motivation for A-TGD. Independent trajectories select only after completion; A-TGD explores early, prunes by likelihood, and completes one trajectory.
Table 8: Reconstruction-module ablation inside the A-TGD outer loop.
Table 9: Annealing-schedule ablation for A-TGD on FFHQ without resampling.
Table 10: Annealing-schedule ablation for A-TGD on FFHQ with always resampling.
Table 7: Ablation of annealing and resampling.
Table 6: Pruning-fraction ablation for A-TGD on FFHQ.

Table 1: Image inverse-problem results at matched wall-clock runtime. DAPS (N = 4) runs four independent trajectories and selects by measurement error; A-TGD starts with four particles and prunes to one trajectory.

Figure 2: Controlled 2D inverse problem. Left: prior context and posterior zoom for a representative test condition. Orange crosses denote the final TGD particles with N = 64; black markers denote the ground-truth clean sample and the sign-ambiguous candidates induced by the absolute-value observation. Right: SWD as a function of particle count. Bands denote ±1 standard error over 10 test conditions. TGD improves rapidly with particle count, while DPS and DPS-DAPS do not benefit from additional independent particles in this setting. Lower is better.

Figure 5: Settings under which existing training-free conditional samplers are recovered as special cases of TGD. Single-stage TGD recovers the chosen conditional reconstruction module, while fully conditioned multi-stage TGD with constant tempering recovers DAPS-style repeated reconstruction and correction.
Table 4: FFHQ phase retrieval speed-quality sweep.
Table 5: Particle-count ablation for A-TGD on FFHQ.

Figure 6: Likelihood-tempering schedules for the EDM discretization with 256 steps. The uniform schedule increases the exponent linearly across outer stages, while the noise-dependent schedules tie the exponent to the EDM noise level sr.

Figure 4: Qualitative reconstructions on FFHQ. We show one inpainting example and one phase retrieval example. A-TGD starts with four particles and prunes to one trajectory, while DAPS (N = 4) runs four independent trajectories and selects the final reconstruction with lowest measurement error.

Figure 3: Speed-quality tradeoff on FFHQ phase retrieval. (a) LPIPS as a function of wall-clock time per image for DPS, DAPS (N = 1), DAPS (N = 4), and A-TGD. (b) Speedup of A-TGD over DAPS (N = 4) to reach fixed LPIPS thresholds.
